name: John Abercrombie
abstract: John Laird Abercrombie (December 16, 1944 – August 22, 2017) was an American jazz guitarist. His work explored jazz fusion, free jazz, and avant-garde jazz.

interviews.title: John Abercrombie interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 19, 2001

concerts.label: Andy LaVerne: A Simple Matter of Conviction

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: I think my first band had John Abercrombie… I had been working with a group with John and Mick Goodrick
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jack DeJohnette
about_quotes.evidence: Chick — he told Anthony Braxton, John Abercrombie, Colin Wolcott. So you know that’s where the contribution is — a contribution of giving like a free concert to raise money for CMS.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack DeJohnette interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 7, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Sheryl Bailey
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...were they doing what John Abercrombie described as finger wiggling...
about_quotes.doc_title: Sheryl Bailey interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: John Abercrombie—he’s got an incredible sense of humor, and I really admire him a lot
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: John Abercrombie, Armen Halburian on percussion, and my girl at that time, Elena, she sang some things... Flying John Abercrombie out for one tune, to L.A., “No problem.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
